Voice of America is tasked with countering authoritarianism through independent news coverage. Summary: The White House proposed a new policy that would require all federal grants to be approved by political appointees who ensure that the money would “demonstrably advance the president’s policy priorities.” The rules would also impose highly prescriptive and political criteria on recipients,  including limits on "issue advocacy" and compliance with "religious liberty laws" among many other requirements.

Source: White House Secretly Swayed Board Meant to Stop Civil Service Politicization - NYT
Citation (MLA): Bromwich, Jonah E. et. al. "White House Secretly Swayed Board Meant to Stop Civil Service Politicization." The New York Times, 28 June 2026. https://www.nytimes.com/2026/06/28/us/politics/trump-firings-workers-merit-systems-protection-board.html
Violation: The article characterized the meeting as a "private push" that was part of a concerted pressure campaign against the Merit Systems Protection Board. The Board is meant to prevent the politicization of the civil service.
Summary: Prior to the Merit Systems Protection Board's ruling that the President is authorized by Article II of the Constitution to dismiss officials without due process, the Trump administration reportedly conducted a pressure campaign on the board. In a job interview with Henry Kerner, the acting chairman of the board, conveyed their belief that the board was bound to follow the Office of Legal Counsel's opinion on Article II cases. In that same meeting, they made a point of saying that Mr. Kerner was not being told how to rule. The final ruling was not exactly on the lines of the OLC's opinion, but resulted in a favorable opinion for the White House.
